# Multi-Tenancy Implementation Scope

> **DECISION (2026-06-15): Option A (shared D1 + `tenantId`) is chosen; paid W4P
> dropped.** The A/B fork below collapses to A — every "B-only" row is removed,
> every "A-only" row is now **required**, and "differs" rows take their A branch.
> The exhaustive isolation contract across all surfaces is
> **`docs/multi-tenancy-isolation-spec.md`** (read that for *what must be true*;
> this doc is *what to change and how big*).

**Target:** Circle/Skool-for-Claude-communities on `claudecommunities.com`, path tenants at `/<slug>` + BYO custom domains.
**Grounds:** `docs/multi-tenancy-plan.md` (v6) against actual code on branch `worktree-multi-tenant`. All file:line refs verified.
**Verified anchors (raw grep, this branch):** 137 `route.ts` under `src/app/api`; 79 `page.tsx`; 38 services in `src/lib/services/*.ts`; 75 models in `prisma/schema.prisma` (zero tenantId); 108 files import `@/lib/region` (the prompt's "285 refs" is ref-count, not file-count); 100 files import `@/lib/prisma`, only **3** import `@/lib/db` (`src/app/community/members/page.tsx`, `src/lib/services/posts.ts`, `src/lib/services/comments.ts`); 53 files reference `getCurrentUser`, splitting into **15 API-route call sites** (no React cache — the hard subset) and **33 page call sites** (auto-memoized); `src/middleware.ts` is 202 lines. Schema constraint census: **27 field-level `@unique`** (50 `@unique` lines − 23 `@@unique`) + **23 composite `@@unique`** + **28 `@@index`** (none on `tenantId`). `wrangler.jsonc` has `DB`/`STORAGE`/workflows/`NOTIFICATIONS` queue/`NEXT_CACHE_DO_QUEUE` DO/`BROWSER` (`:98`) but **no** `dispatch_namespace`, **no** `PLATFORM_DB`. Spike present at `spike/phase0/` with `shared/protocol.ts` + `shared/remote-d1.ts` + `data-worker/`.

---

## 1. Executive summary

We are converting a single-tenant, region-hardcoded Next.js-on-Cloudflare app into a multi-tenant SaaS where each community is a tenant addressed by path (`claudecommunities.com/india`) or custom domain, self-provisioned in minutes.

**The one open decision is the data plane:**
- **Option A — shared D1 + `tenantId` column** + a Prisma `$extends` chokepoint that auto-injects/filters `tenantId`.
- **Option B — per-tenant D1** via Workers-for-Platforms dispatch namespace, reached through a session-multiplexed remote-D1 transport (one dispatch session per request, queries as pipelined frames).

**Recommendation: Option B.** The Phase 0 spike already proves B's transport is byte-identical to a native D1 binding (P2002 text, `Bytes` round-trip, `$transaction`, `meta.changes`) and collapses ~16 queries into 1 invocation — which is exactly what the 32-invocation request-chain cap forces. B gives true per-tenant DB isolation (the requirement in plan §1.3) without the silent-fail risk of a `$extends` filter that, if it ever misses, leaks cross-tenant data unscoped. A is the documented fallback if the spike fails real-world W4P billing/latency.

**~90% of the work is invariant across A/B.** The chokepoint, the `/t/[tenant]` restructure, the `region.ts → getTenantConfig()` migration, the storage-prefix fix, the identity/MCP rules, the cache-tag scoping, the queue/cron fan-out, and all CI isolation tests are identical either way. The data plane only changes: the body of `getPrisma()` (RemoteD1 session vs `$extends`), whether the ~38 tenant-scoped models gain a `tenantId` column + composite uniques/indexes, and a handful of `wrangler.jsonc` bindings (`TENANTS` namespace vs none).

---

## 2. The chokepoint first — the keystone

Everything hinges on one new module landing before any other tenant work: **`src/lib/tenant-context.ts`**, exposing `getTenantContext()` and a rewritten **`src/lib/prisma.ts`** exposing `getPrisma()` / `getPlatformPrisma()`.

Why it is the keystone:

- **It makes the data plane swappable.** Every consumer calls `getPrisma()`; only that function's body differs between A and B. Decide A vs B late, behind a stable interface, without touching the 100 importers again.
- **It is the security boundary.** Tenant resolves *exactly once per request*, from the URL/host chokepoint (middleware rewrite `/t/<slug>/…` + internal header), **never from the Clerk JWT org claim** (plan §6.2 — sessions have one active org; two tabs flip it globally and would authorize the wrong tenant). Memoization must be keyed per `(ctx, tenant)`, never per-ctx alone.
- **It must respect the ALS reality** (plan §4.2): an AsyncLocalStorage scope entered in Next middleware does **not** survive into render. So `getTenantContext()` derives lazily from `headers()`/route params on first data-layer access, and **every non-HTTP entry** (workflow steps, queue consumers, `scheduled()`, **and the OpenNext ISR `DOQueueHandler` re-render path**) calls `runWithTenant(tenantId, fn)` explicitly via the existing `src/lib/cf-env.ts` ALS bridge. The DO revalidation queue (`NEXT_CACHE_DO_QUEUE`, `wrangler.jsonc:87`) re-renders tenant pages out of request scope and is easy to miss — it is enumerated in §3.6.

Until this lands, nothing else can be correctly tenant-scoped. It is the first deliverable of Phase 1 and gates the rest.

---

## 3. Work breakdown by subsystem

### 3.1 Data-plane chokepoint (the keystone)
**Current:** `src/lib/prisma.ts:30` memoizes a tenant-unaware client on a `WeakMap` keyed by Cloudflare ctx; default export is a Proxy (`:61`) bound to `env.DB` via `PrismaD1` (`:53`). No tenant context anywhere. 3 raw-SQL files bypass even that.

| Change | Files | Effort | Data plane |
|---|---|---|---|
| New `getTenantContext()` — lazy resolve from rewrite/header, memoize per `(ctx,tenant)`, return `{slug, session, dbSession}` | `src/lib/tenant-context.ts` | M | invariant |
| `runWithTenant()` ALS for non-HTTP entries, parallel to `workflowEnvALS` | `src/lib/cf-env.ts` | S | invariant |
| Rewrite `prisma.ts`: add `getPrisma()` (tenant) + `getPlatformPrisma()` (`PLATFORM_DB`); keep default export Proxy → `getPrisma()` for back-compat | `src/lib/prisma.ts` | M | **differs** (RemoteD1 session vs `$extends`) |
| `getRemoteD1Session(slug)` — one session per request to `env.TENANTS.get(slug)`; pipeline frames | `src/lib/tenant-context.ts` | L | **B-only** |
| Migrate 3 raw-SQL callers off `@/lib/db` to `getPrisma()` | `src/app/community/members/page.tsx`, `src/lib/services/posts.ts`, `src/lib/services/comments.ts` | S | invariant |
| **[A only] Close the raw-SQL hole structurally.** A's `$extends` filter operates on the Prisma query AST — it is **blind to** `$queryRaw`/`$executeRaw` and any surviving D1 `prepare()`. Migrating the 3 `@/lib/db` callers is necessary but not sufficient: ban `$queryRaw`/`$executeRaw`/`$queryRawUnsafe`/`$executeRawUnsafe` via ESLint, OR route them through a wrapper that injects `tenantId` into the SQL+binds. Under B this hole does not exist — every statement targets the tenant's own DB. | `eslint.config.mjs`, any raw-SQL call site | S | **A-only** |
| ESLint lockdown: ban `env.TENANTS`/`env.DB`/`env.PLATFORM_DB` outside `tenant-context.ts`/`prisma.ts`/`cf-env.ts`; require `getPrisma()`/`getPlatformPrisma()` not default import | `eslint.config.mjs` | S | invariant |

| Change | Files | Effort | Data plane |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Normalize-THEN-match** middleware: extract host+path → resolve tenant (slug or custom-domain registry lookup, cached ~30s) → rewrite `/t/<slug>/…` + header → run allowlists/Clerk/maintenance against normalized path | `src/middleware.ts` | M | invariant |
| `(platform)` route group for apex surfaces (directory/marketing home, `/start`, platform `/admin`, legal, `.well-known`) | `src/app/(platform)/` | M | invariant |
| Move **entire app** under `src/app/t/[tenant]/…` (admin, events, courses, community, my-talks, cities, merch + ~120 API routes); keep ~30 at apex (webhooks, cron, internal, `.well-known`) | `src/app/t/[tenant]/`, `src/app/api/t/[tenant]/` | **XL** | invariant |
| Reserved-slug policy from allowlist literals (admin, api, start, sign-in, t, _next, robots, sitemap, resources, speak, pricing, cowork, merch, mcp, impact-lab, internal, webinars, cities, community, courses, events, …) | `src/lib/slug-policy.ts`, `docs/reserved-slugs.md` | S | invariant |
| `tenantHref()` — render links from registry canonical base URL, not request host (ISR/email correctness); convert ~180 `href="/"` | `src/lib/tenant-href.ts`, `src/components/**`, `src/app/t/[tenant]/**` | L | invariant |
| Per-tenant MCP + RFC 9728 discovery under `/t/[tenant]`; apex MCP stays as platform discovery; tenant context from URL, **not** token org claim (see §3.4 for the membership-rejection rule) | `src/app/t/[tenant]/api/mcp/route.ts`, `.well-known/oauth-protected-resource`, `src/lib/mcp/tools.ts` | M | invariant |

| Change | Files | Effort | Data plane |

| Cities → per-tenant enablement; keep `ALL_CITIES` static reference | `src/lib/cities.ts`, `src/lib/services/events.ts`, `src/app/cities/[slug]/page.tsx` | L | shared-mostly |
| Delete `src/lib/region.ts`; `siteUrl()`/`appUrl()` become async wrappers; `getPlatformConfig()` for apex | `src/lib/region.ts` (delete) | — | invariant |

### 3.3a ISR cache tags & `unstable_cache` keys — cross-tenant cache bleed (NEW)
**Current:** `CACHE_TAGS` in `src/lib/revalidate.ts:18` are **literal global strings** (`posts: "posts"`, `events: "events"`). Verified usage is **4 files**: `revalidate.ts` (defs), `src/app/community/page.tsx`, `src/lib/services/posts.ts`, `src/lib/services/events.ts`. `community/page.tsx` holds **6 `unstable_cache` entries** — two tagged (`getUpcomingEventCount`/`getPostCount`/`getTrendingPosts` carry `CACHE_TAGS.*`, lines 104/112/151) and **four untagged** (`getSpaces:18`, `getMemberStats:29`, `getOnlineMembers:154`, `getTopContributors:154+`). `courses.ts`/`scheduled-courses.ts` have **zero** cache usage (the critic's "9 files" was overstated).

Under a shared serving worker this is a live cross-tenant defect on **two** axes:
1. **Tag axis:** tenant A's `revalidateTag("posts")` busts tenant B's cached `posts` entries.
2. **Key axis (the more dangerous one):** `unstable_cache` collisions are keyed on the **cache key**, not the tag. The four *untagged* entries above (e.g. `getSpaces`) collide across tenants → **one tenant serves another tenant's cached spaces/members/contributors**. Tag-prefixing alone does not fix these; the cache *key* must be tenant-scoped.

**Rule (invariant, both A and B):** every `CACHE_TAGS` value AND every `unstable_cache` keyParts array gains a `tenant:<slug>:` prefix. Introduce `tenantTag(slug, name)` and `tenantKey(slug, …parts)` helpers in `revalidate.ts`; thread `slug` from `getTenantContext()` into all 4 files. `revalidateTag`/`revalidatePath` calls in service write paths must resolve the current tenant (or accept it explicitly when called from `runWithTenant` contexts).

| Change | Files | Effort | Data plane |
|---|---|---|---|
| `tenantTag()`/`tenantKey()` helpers; convert `CACHE_TAGS` to a `(slug)`-parameterized factory | `src/lib/revalidate.ts:18` | S | invariant |
| Prefix every `unstable_cache` keyParts **and** `tags` (incl. the 4 untagged entries) with `tenant:<slug>:` | `src/app/community/page.tsx` (6 entries), `src/lib/services/posts.ts`, `src/lib/services/events.ts` | M | invariant |
| Tenant-resolve `revalidateTag`/`revalidatePath` call sites in service write paths (and the non-request `*Safe` paths, which must take an explicit slug) | `src/lib/services/posts.ts`, `events.ts`, `revalidate.ts` | S | invariant |

### 3.4 Identity & permissions
**Current:** `src/lib/auth.ts` — `resolveSessionUser()` (:27) → `getCurrentUser = cache(resolveSessionUser)` (:134); finds user by `clerkId` (:35), email fallback at **:71** with `where: { email: { equals: email } }` — **`equals` is case-sensitive on D1/SQLite, so the "case-insensitive" comment at :68 is false; this is a live bug** (imported/invited users with differing case silently fail to match). P2002 race handler at :110. One Clerk app (`app_39H1P4btQo3xef4S3DsfanaaMmo` per `docs/auth.md`), prod+dev instances, no orgs. `src/lib/permissions.ts` Role table (`name @id`), `getRolePermissions()`. `src/lib/mcp/tools.ts` `getDbUser()` trusts `authInfo.extra.userId`, **no tenant-membership check**.

| Change | Files | Effort | Data plane |
|---|---|---|---|
| Thread tenant into `getCurrentUser`/`ensureUserInDb`; memoize per `(tenant,userId)`; validate membership against URL tenant not JWT org | `src/lib/auth.ts`, `src/lib/tenant-context.ts` | M | **differs (membership read path — see note below)** |
| Clerk Organizations: org per tenant on approval; coarse role (owner/admin/member) in Clerk, fine-grained Role stays in tenant DB | `src/lib/clerk-roles.ts`, `src/lib/auth.ts`, schema | M | invariant |
| **Phase-0 fix:** case-insensitive email match (raw `LOWER(email) = LOWER(?)` or a normalized lowercase column) at `auth.ts:71` | `src/lib/auth.ts` | S | invariant |
| `PendingAdminGrant` consumed at first sign-in (lowercase email) | `src/lib/auth.ts`, schema | S | invariant |
| Platform admins via `publicMetadata.platformRole`; gate apex `/admin` | `src/lib/auth.ts`, `src/middleware.ts`, `(platform)/admin/layout.tsx` | S | invariant |
| **MCP membership assertion (NEW, security-critical):** resolve tenant from URL; in `getDbUser()`, after looking up user X from `authInfo.extra.userId`, **reject (401/403) if X has no membership row for the URL tenant.** Without this, any authenticated token reaches any tenant's MCP. Per-tenant endpoint alone does not enforce this. | `src/lib/mcp/tools.ts` | M | invariant |
| Migrate 53 `getCurrentUser` references. **Blast radius split: 33 page call sites stay auto-memoized via `cache()`; the hard subset is the 15 API-route call sites** (route handlers have no React cache), which must take explicit tenant context or run inside `runWithTenant`. | `src/lib/auth.ts` + callers | L | invariant |

**Why the membership read path "differs" between A and B (the authorization boundary):** Membership lives in `UserTenant`. Under **B**, that table is in the tenant's own DB, reached via the dispatch session — a query against the wrong tenant's DB simply returns no row, so isolation is structural. Under **A**, `UserTenant` is a shared-table row filtered by the same `$extends` predicate that scopes everything else; if that predicate ever misses (the documented silent-fail risk), a membership check could read or match the wrong tenant's rows. This is the single most important place where A's `$extends` correctness is load-bearing for *authorization*, not just data partitioning — hence the explicit "differs" marking and the §3.1 raw-SQL ban (a membership check that fell through to raw SQL under A would be unscoped).

### 3.5 Schema & data model
**Current:** single D1 `claudecommunity-db` (`wrangler.jsonc:54`); 75 models, zero tenantId; **27 field-level `@unique`** + 23 composite `@@unique` + 28 `@@index` (none on `tenantId`). SQLite can't `ALTER ADD UNIQUE`/`ADD INDEX` cheaply — every rescope is CREATE→COPY→DROP→RENAME.

| Change | Files | Effort | Data plane |
|---|---|---|---|
| New `Tenant` (registry), `TenantSetting`, `UserTenant` (membership join — User can't carry tenantId), `PendingAdminGrant`, `AuditLog` | `prisma/schema.prisma` (B: `PLATFORM_DB` for `Tenant`/registry) | M | **registry is invariant-platform — see note** |
| **[A only]** Add `tenantId` to the ~38 tenant-scoped models; expand-contract migration (nullable→backfill→NOT NULL) | `prisma/schema.prisma`, `migrations/` | L | **A-only** |
| **[A only] Composite indexes, not single-column.** A bare `@@index([tenantId])` is near-useless because every hot query filters by tenant **plus** an existing key. Each new index must be `@@index([tenantId, …existing])`, e.g. `[tenantId, spaceId]` (posts), `[tenantId, eventId, order]`, `[tenantId, status, nextActionAt]` (workflow drains), mirroring the existing 28 single-tenant indexes with `tenantId` prepended. | `prisma/schema.prisma`, `migrations/` | M | **A-only** |
| **[A only]** Rescope the **tenant-scoped field-uniques** to composite (audit below) | `prisma/schema.prisma`, `migrations/` | M | **A-only** |
| **[A only]** `Role.name @id` (`:89`) → `(tenantId,name)` composite PK (largest single migration; reshapes the PK that `getRolePermissions()` joins on) | `prisma/schema.prisma` | M | **A-only** |
| **[B]** No per-model schema change — template schema identical across tenant DBs; the 5 new platform-registry models live in `PLATFORM_DB` | `prisma/schema.prisma` | — | **B-only** |

**Registry location is invariant, not A/B-divergent (correction).** The custom-domain → tenant and slug → tenant registry **must live in `PLATFORM_DB` under both A and B** — you cannot resolve *which* tenant DB (B) or *which* `tenantId` filter (A) to use from inside a tenant DB; the lookup is circular. So `Tenant` + the custom-domain map are platform-plane under either option. What differs is only where the *per-tenant* tables live (shared table+`tenantId` under A vs separate DB under B).

**Field-unique audit (the load-bearing A-path artifact).** Classification of all 27 field-`@unique` columns into **identity-global** (stay global — these key on a person or a platform-wide token, not a community) vs **tenant-scoped** (rescope to `@@unique([tenantId, col])` under A):

- **Identity-global (NO rescope):** `User.clerkId` (:12), `User.email` (:14), `EmailSuppressionList.email`, email/tracking opaque tokens, and any column whose value is globally unique by construction (unsubscribe tokens, verification tokens). A person and their suppression status are platform-wide.
- **Tenant-scoped (rescope to composite under A):** `Space.slug` (:121), `Event.slug` (:209), `Role.name` (:89, also the PK reshape above), `MembershipTier.name` (:502) + `MembershipTier.slug` (:503), `MembershipLevel.level` (:581), `Badge.name` (:89-region), and the remaining content-namespace slugs/names. These are unique *within a community*, not globally — two tenants must each be able to have a `#general` space or a `member` role.
- **Already composite — leave alone (the draft's earlier guess was wrong):** `SocialAccount` is already `@@unique([connector, externalId])` (`:1186`) — but note its semantics: under per-tenant scope it should become `@@unique([tenantId, connector, externalId])` under A so two tenants can connect the same LinkedIn org id; under B it is naturally per-DB. `Course`/lesson are already `@@unique([courseId, slug])` (`:470`, `:497`) — `courseId` is itself tenant-scoped, so these need no further change beyond `Course`'s own slug (`:439`) being rescoped.

The ~38-vs-37 tenant-scoped/global model split is decided by the **same boundary rule**: a model is tenant-scoped if its rows belong to a single community (Space, Post, Comment, Event, Course, MembershipTier, Role, social posts, email campaigns, …); it is global if it keys on a person's platform identity or a platform-wide artifact (User, EmailSuppressionList, MembershipLevel-as-catalog if shared, suppression/tracking tokens). The per-model list is produced as the first deliverable of the §3.5 [A-only] block and is the gate for the expand-contract migrations.

### 3.6 Storage, slide-render, cron/workflows, MCP, bindings
**Current:** `folder` attacker-controlled at `upload/route.ts:7,31` + `upload/mcp/route.ts` + `api/files/[...key]/route.ts` unscoped. `src/worker-scheduled.ts` producer-only, cron `*/15` (`wrangler.jsonc:140`); `scripts/inject-workflow-exports.mjs` injects `scheduled()` only — **no `queue()` consumer**. Workflows carry no tenantId. `NEXT_CACHE_DO_QUEUE`/`DOQueueHandler` (`:87`) re-renders ISR pages out of request scope. `NOTIFICATIONS` queue (`:107`) produces to `app-notifications`, **consumed by a separate `cloudflare-build-notifications` worker outside this repo** (`:102`). `BROWSER` binding (`:98`) drives slide PNG export.

**Slide-render / Browser Rendering (NEW subsystem — was missing entirely).** `src/lib/services/slideRender.ts:307` does `puppeteer.launch(getBrowserBinding())` and navigates an **HMAC-signed render URL** (signed by `src/lib/slideRenderSign.ts:68`, secret `RENDER_SIGNING_SECRET`). The headless page hits the app **with no Clerk session**, so under path tenants:
1. The signed render URL must include the tenant segment: `…/t/<slug>/admin/slides/render/<hash>.png`. The signed payload (`eventId|slideId|speakerId|refWidth|exp`) gains the tenant slug so the signature binds the URL to the tenant; the render route resolves tenant **from the URL path only** (no header, no JWT — the puppeteer request carries neither).
2. **Decision flagged:** per-tenant signing secret vs one shared `RENDER_SIGNING_SECRET`. A shared secret + tenant-in-payload is sufficient for integrity if the payload is signed; a per-tenant secret is stronger isolation but adds a secret-management surface per tenant. Recommend shared secret + tenant in signed payload for P1, revisit in P5 with BYO keys.
3. The render route's `getPrisma()`/`getTenantContext()` therefore must accept URL-derived tenant identity exactly as the public path tenants do.

| Change | Files | Effort | Data plane |
|---|---|---|---|
| Storage write chokepoint: derive `t/<slug>/` from context; client `folder` = sanitized sub-path only | `src/lib/storage.ts:146`, `src/app/api/upload/route.ts:7,31`, `upload/mcp/route.ts` | M | invariant (R2 always shared) |
| **Storage read authorization (NEW, the actual control):** `api/files/[...key]/route.ts` must **require the key's `t/<slug>/` prefix to equal the request's tenant**, not merely block traversal. A tenant-A request for `t/<slugB>/…` is rejected even if the key is valid and guessed correctly. | `src/app/api/files/[...key]/route.ts` | S | invariant |
| Rate-limit + ISR keys include tenantId | upload routes | S | invariant |
| Workflow payloads carry `tenantId`; steps enter via `runWithTenant`; extend injector to emit `queue()` | `src/workflows/*.ts`, `scripts/inject-workflow-exports.mjs` | M | invariant |
| **DO ISR revalidation (`NEXT_CACHE_DO_QUEUE`/`DOQueueHandler`, `:87`) enters `runWithTenant`** — it re-renders tenant pages out of request scope; without tenant context it resolves the wrong/no tenant. Tenant slug must be encoded in the cache key (see §3.3a) so the DO re-render targets the right tenant. | `open-next.config.*`, ISR cache-key plumbing | M | invariant |
| Cron fan-out: `scheduled()` enumerates live tenants → enqueue 1 `tenant-jobs` msg/tenant; consumer drains in own 32-invocation budget; chunk ≤25 | `src/worker-scheduled.ts`, `wrangler.jsonc` | L | **B-leaning** |
| **Notifications tenant-tagging + external-consumer contract (NEW detail):** tag every `app-notifications` message body with `tenantId` (fixes the NZ-dropped defect). **The consumer is a separate worker (`cloudflare-build-notifications`) outside this repo** — tagging the body is necessary but the routing/identity *contract* with that worker must be defined: it must read `tenantId` from the body and route per-tenant (channel/webhook), and it must not assume a single region. This is a cross-repo coordination item, not a same-repo edit. | `src/lib/notify.ts` (+ external worker contract doc) | S (+coordination) | invariant |
| `wrangler.jsonc`: add `PLATFORM_DB`; **[B]** add `TENANTS` dispatch namespace + `PROVISION`/`tenant-jobs` queues; provisioning worker (`workers/tenant-data/`, separate non-routable worker holds CF API token) | `wrangler.jsonc`, `workers/tenant-data/` | A:S / B:XL | **differs** |

---

## 4. The big mechanical migrations (slicing strategy)

Three changes dominate the churn. Each gets a deliberate codemod-vs-manual split.

**(a) Region → tenant config — 108 files / ~285 refs.**
- *Codemod-able (~70%):* `getRegionConfig()` → `await getTenantConfig()` in RSC/route/server contexts; wrap `export const metadata` → `async generateMetadata`. jscodeshift transform, ~2–4h to write+test.
- *Manual (~30%):* client components can't `await` — hoist config to props from the nearest RSC parent (~13 components); `src/lib/resend.ts` and `src/workflows/campaign-send.ts` move from module-load inlining to per-call lookup; `src/middleware.ts:29` keeps a static platform fallback for maintenance HTML.
- *Gate:* build fails if any client component calls the async fn — that's your manual-fix worklist.

**(b) Prisma importers — 100 files + 53 `getCurrentUser` references (15 API-route call sites + 33 page call sites).**
- *Semi-codemod:* swap `import prisma from "@/lib/prisma"` default → `getPrisma()` and await. **Not blind** — spot-audit 10–20 to classify tenant (`getPrisma`, ~99%) vs platform (`getPlatformPrisma`: Clerk user ops, platform admin). ESLint lockdown enforces the choice afterward.
- *Manual:* the 3 raw-`@/lib/db` callers; the **15 API-route `getCurrentUser` call sites** (no React cache → explicit context/`runWithTenant`); `auth.ts` (memoization key change + the `:71` email fix); any platform-only table access. The 33 page call sites stay auto-memoized via `cache()` and only need the tenant key wired through `getTenantContext()`.
- *Slice order:* `/admin` first → `/api/posts` → `/events` → rest, with cross-tenant isolation test after each batch.

**(c) ~38-model tenantId + unique/index-scoping — A-only.**
- *Mechanical bulk:* add `tenantId` + **composite** `@@index([tenantId, …existing])` to ~38 models, batched into 2–3 migrations by domain (spaces→posts→comments; events; courses; email; social).
- *Hand-written:* the tenant-scoped field-unique rescopes from the §3.5 audit + the `Role.name`→`(tenantId,name)` PK reshape (each a SQLite table-rebuild migration); expand-contract backfill scripts.
- **If B is chosen this entire block evaporates** — the template schema is shared across per-tenant DBs and only the 5 new platform-registry models are added to `PLATFORM_DB`.

---

## 5. Phased plan (each phase shippable)

Maps to plan §12, grounded in this code.

**Phase 0 — Spike + foundations (go/no-go gate). Size L.**
- Validate the multiplexed session transport on deployed W4P: 32-invocation rule, conformance corpus (P2002 text, `Bytes`, `$transaction`, `meta.changes`) byte-identical vs native binding, CPU-ms billing, in-flight survival on template re-upload. (`spike/phase0/` already proves transport *locally*.)
- **Cleanup fixes that belong here:** `auth.ts:71` case-insensitive email (real bug); `upload/route.ts:7,31` `folder` sanitization can land now as defense-in-depth (full tenant prefix waits for context in P1).
- Slug/reserved policy; LinkedIn Standard-tier application filed; Clerk org-model spike; "who pays" decision.
- **Exit:** spike answers A-vs-B definitively; if it fails, fall back to A (or v4 direct-bindings).

**Phase 1 — Tenant-aware core. Size XL.**
- Chokepoint (`tenant-context.ts`, `getPrisma`/`getPlatformPrisma`, `runWithTenant` incl. the DO-revalidation entry); normalize-then-match middleware; `(platform)` + `/t/[tenant]` restructure; `tenantHref()`; storage write-prefix + **read-authorization** enforcement; **cache-tag + `unstable_cache`-key tenant scoping (§3.3a)**; **slide-render signed-URL tenant segment (§3.6)**; `region.ts` deletion + 108-file migration; registry (`PLATFORM_DB`) + `TenantSetting` + validation; identity rules of §3.4 incl. the **MCP membership assertion**; 3 raw-SQL migrations + (A) the `$queryRaw` ban; ESLint lockdown; cross-tenant CI tests (multi-tenant-in-one-request, cross-tab org claim, storage read-prefix escape, MCP cross-tenant token rejection, cache-key bleed).
- **Exit:** AU runs as a path tenant on staging end-to-end through a data-worker session (B) / tenantId-scoped client (A).

**Phase 2 — Platform plane. Size L.**
- Provisioning worker (non-routable, holds CF API token) + `PROVISION`/`tenant-jobs` queues + consumer wiring (extend `inject-workflow-exports.mjs`); `TENANT_PROVISION` workflow; `/start` signup + approvals; platform `/admin` (approve/suspend/admins/fleet-with-chunked-health/audit); tenant-aware cron fan-out; **notifications tenant-tagging + the external `cloudflare-build-notifications` routing contract**; template lifecycle (versioning, canary, drift `repair`); migration fan-out + version gate; KEK escrow.
- **Exit:** self-serve provision → live in minutes on staging.

**Phase 3 — Custom domains. Size L.** Cloudflare for SaaS (fallback origin, zone route, O2O playbook); attach flow + cert UI; Clerk satellite domains; canonical-URL + domain-event wholesale revalidation.

**Phase 4 — AU/NZ migration. Size L.** §10: seed AU/NZ tenants; `wrangler d1 export`→import; R2 copy + legacy-alias map; domain cutover; retire NZ account + per-region deploys. Full staging rehearsal mandatory.
**clerkId rewrite — mechanism (contradiction resolved).** There is no contradiction with §1's "one Clerk app + orgs." Today AU and NZ deploy to **separate Cloudflare accounts with separate secrets** (`.env.nz.*`); `docs/auth.md` documents only the AU Clerk app (`app_39H1P4…`, prod+dev instances), and NZ's Clerk credentials live outside this repo — i.e. **AU and NZ run on two distinct Clerk instances today**. Unifying to one app + orgs (§3.4) therefore re-keys **one region's** users into the canonical app: the canonical region's users keep their `clerkId` and simply gain org memberships (no rewrite), while the **other region's users get new `clerkId`s in the canonical app** — that is exactly what the rewrite map maps (old-region-clerkId → new-canonical-clerkId), applied across every `User.clerkId` (`:12`) and FK reference. This is the riskiest step; the fallback is "user signs in again" (Clerk re-issues identity, email-match via the now-fixed case-insensitive `auth.ts:71` lookup re-links them to their existing DB row). If, contrary to the deploy topology, AU and NZ turn out to share one instance, the map is unnecessary and this step collapses to org-membership backfill only — confirm instance identity in `docs/auth.md`/NZ secrets at the start of P4.

**Phase 5 — SaaS polish. Size M.** Tenant quotas + W4P custom limits; BYO encrypted keys (incl. optional per-tenant `RENDER_SIGNING_SECRET`); offboarding/export; billing; apex directory/discovery.

---

## 6. Total effort & critical path

Order-of-magnitude (eng-weeks, one experienced engineer, includes test/review):

| Phase | Size | ~Weeks |
|---|---|---|
| 0 Spike | L | 3–4 |
| 1 Core | XL | 8–12 |
| 2 Platform | L | 4–6 |
| 3 Domains | L | 3–5 |
| 4 AU/NZ | L | 3–5 |
| 5 Polish | M | 2–4 |
| **Total** | | **~23–36 weeks** |

Phases 0–2 (~15–22 weeks) deliver the product promise (self-serve path tenants).

**Critical path:** Phase 0 spike → **chokepoint (`tenant-context.ts` + `getPrisma`)** → `/t/[tenant]` restructure → everything else. The chokepoint is the single hard dependency; the restructure is the longest single task (137 routes + 79 pages). Choosing B adds the per-tenant DB transport (already spiked) but **removes** the entire ~38-model `tenantId`/unique/composite-index scoping block (~3–4 weeks of fiddly SQLite rebuilds) **and** the `$queryRaw` raw-SQL ban, so B is not materially slower than A on the critical path and is safer.

---

## 7. Open decisions (deduped)

1. **Data plane A vs B.** Tradeoff: A = simpler infra, but a `$extends` filter that silently leaks if it ever misses (and is **structurally blind to raw SQL** and load-bearing on the *membership/authorization* read path) + costly ~38-model SQLite migrations; B = true per-DB isolation + spiked transport, but W4P CPU-ms billing and the 32-invocation discipline. **→ B**, gated on Phase 0 billing/latency; A is the fallback.
2. **Apex `/` behavior.** Redirect to platform `/admin`, or a Skool-style directory? **→** Build a thin directory in P5; until then apex `/` = `/start` + sign-in. Determines whether today's AU homepage moves verbatim to `/t/au` (it should — it's tenant content).
3. **Custom-domain / tenant registry location.** Per-tenant lookups are circular. **→ `PLATFORM_DB` under both A and B** (invariant-platform), in-isolate cached (~30s TTL).
4. **User-scoped data (EmailPreference, Activity, Notification, Connection): global or per-tenant?** **→ per-tenant** (matches "separate DB per tenant"; under B they live in each tenant DB naturally). Connection (cross-user) needs a call — recommend per-tenant.
5. **Email campaigns / SocialAccount / ScheduledCourse / ImpactLab scope.** ImpactLab is hardcoded to one event (`@default("melbourne-may-2026")`) — **→ keep platform-level for now**, revisit if multi-city. SocialAccount/campaigns **→ per-tenant** (each community posts/sends as itself; under A, `SocialAccount` composite unique extends to `[tenantId, connector, externalId]`). ScheduledCourse **→ per-tenant**.
6. **`getCurrentUser` signature.** Optional `tenantId` arg vs ALS-only. **→** auto-derive from `getTenantContext()`; memoize per `(tenant,userId)`; explicit `runWithTenant` for non-HTTP. The **15 API-route call sites** (no React cache) get explicit context; the 33 page call sites stay memoized.
7. **Legacy-URL handling in P1.** 301 stub at apex `/admin`,`/events` → `/t/au/…`? **→** include a redirect stub in P1 so staging cutover doesn't 404; final domain canonicalization is P3/P4.
8. **`/impact-lab` routing.** Own auth, AU-specific today. **→** keep at apex (platform-shared) until multi-city; do not block P1.
9. **Sitemap/robots.** **→** per-tenant under `/t/[tenant]/sitemap.xml`; apex serves a directory-index sitemap + apex robots for legacy crawlers.
10. **Tenant pricing.** Free while invite-only vs Stripe day one. **→** free/invite-only through P4; billing in P5. (Clerk "unlimited org members" paid tier is the real cost lever, not our billing.)
11. **Slide-render signing secret.** Shared `RENDER_SIGNING_SECRET` + tenant-in-signed-payload vs per-tenant secret. **→** shared + tenant-in-payload for P1; per-tenant secret optional in P5 (BYO keys).
12. **Notifications external-consumer contract.** The `cloudflare-build-notifications` worker is out-of-repo. **→** define the `tenantId`-in-body routing contract as a P2 coordination item before relying on per-tenant notification delivery.

**Key files to anchor implementation:** chokepoint `src/lib/tenant-context.ts` (new) + `src/lib/prisma.ts:30,53,61`; middleware `src/middleware.ts:62,98,134`; config `src/lib/region.ts:74,151,160`; cache `src/lib/revalidate.ts:18` + `src/app/community/page.tsx:18,29,104,112,151,154`; identity `src/lib/auth.ts:68,71,110,134` + `src/lib/mcp/tools.ts` (`getDbUser`); storage `src/app/api/upload/route.ts:7,31` + `src/lib/storage.ts:146` + `src/app/api/files/[...key]/route.ts`; slide-render `src/lib/services/slideRender.ts:307` + `src/lib/slideRenderSign.ts:68` + `wrangler.jsonc:98`; cron/queues/DO `src/worker-scheduled.ts` + `scripts/inject-workflow-exports.mjs` + `wrangler.jsonc:87,107`; schema `prisma/schema.prisma:12,14,89,121,209,439,470,502,503,581,1186`; bindings `wrangler.jsonc:54`; spike reference `spike/phase0/shared/{protocol,remote-d1}.ts`.
